commands.review_summary:
The PR adds one-time managed npm root quarantine/retry for npm tree-mutation failures, downgrades post-core plugin convergence failures to warnings so update can restart Gateway, and updates CLI docs/tests.

PR surface: Source +113, Tests +162, Docs 0. Total +275 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Not as a live current-main run in this review; the linked issue logs plus current-main source make the failure path source-reproducible, but the PR still needs real packaged update proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed for the fail-open update semantics and the contributor must supply real update/Gateway proof that automation cannot provide for their setup.

target.title: [codex] fix update plugin failure availability
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the update path that can leave Gateway down after a post-core plugin install failure.

## Root Cause

A managed npm plugin install can fail with local npm tree corruption signatures such as `ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE` or `ENOTEMPTY` while mutating `node_modules`. The updater then converted plugin-scoped post-core convergence failures into a top-level update error, so the package update could finish but the managed Gateway restart path was skipped.

## Changes

- Detect npm managed-root corruption signatures, including `ENOTEMPTY` tree mutation failures.
- Quarantine only rebuild artifacts (`node_modules`, `package-lock.json`, `npm-shrinkwrap.json`) and retry the managed npm install once.
- Keep post-core plugin convergence failures as plugin warnings so the updated Gateway still restarts.
- Update CLI docs for the new behavior.

Related: #85113

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: npm-managed plugin installs now recognize the `ENOTEMPTY` local tree corruption shape and move only rebuild artifacts out of the managed npm project before retrying, instead of leaving the managed plugin tree wedged.
- Real environment tested: local macOS checkout at PR head using the patched source and a real filesystem-backed OpenClaw managed npm project directory. No mocked test runner was used for this proof command.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` from the repo root with a script that imports `src/infra/npm-managed-root.ts`, creates a managed npm project containing stale `@openclaw/discord/node_modules/undici/types` payload plus `package-lock.json`, calls `shouldRebuildManagedNpmRootAfterInstallFailure`, then calls `quarantineManagedNpmRootForRebuild`.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
detected=true
moved=node_modules,package-lock.json
node_modules_exists_after=false
package_lock_exists_after=false
package_json_preserved=true
archive_preserved=true
stale_payload_quarantined=true
```

- Observed result after fix: the exact `ENOTEMPTY` signature was classified for rebuild, `node_modules` and `package-lock.json` were moved into `_openclaw-quarantined-npm-roots`, `package.json` and `_openclaw-pack-archives` remained in place for the retry, and the stale Discord/undici payload was preserved in quarantine.
- What was not tested: the full Docker corrupt-plugin update E2E could not run in this local environment because Docker is not installed (`env: docker: No such file or directory`). The package build and tarball integrity phase completed before Docker startup failed.

## Validation

- `pnpm test src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/infra/npm-managed-root.ts src/plugins/install.ts src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`
- Attempted `pnpm test:docker:update-corrupt-plugin`; package build and tarball integrity passed, then the lane stopped before the scenario because Docker is unavailable locally.
